summary: Joe Reis reflects on ChatGPT's third anniversary, noting how it unexpectedly became the fastest-growing app in history and fundamentally shifted the data industry's focus from topics like Data Mesh to AI. He observes that while the technology has evolved from crude chatbots to agents and context engineering, the core innovation continues to deliver real utility despite the hype cycles.
key_points: ChatGPT launched expecting 10,000 users but reached 100 million by January 2023
The data industry's focus shifted dramatically from Data Mesh to AI by mid-2023
AI technology has evolved from chatbots to agents, with context engineering replacing prompt engineering
Despite hype cycles and expertise bandwagons, the underlying AI innovation provides genuine utility
money_quotes: A friend who was on the original team that created and launched ChatGPT told me they figured that maybe 10,000 people would use the app. By January 2023, over 100 million people were using ChatGPT, making it the fastest-growing app in history.
Just like everyone was an AI expert a couple of years ago, now it seems like everyone is an expert in ontologies and knowledge graphs. So it goes.
Even if the current AI bubble pops, it won't stop innovation in next-generation AI.
Whether people love or hate ChatGPT, there's no denying that it has changed the world.
key_insight: ChatGPT's launch marked a before-and-after moment that permanently shifted the tech industry's trajectory, and despite the inevitable hype cycles and expertise bandwagons, the underlying innovation delivers real value that won't disappear even if the bubble pops.
